feedback.text: Hello, I am a video game player (or gamer) from the Netherlands and I see this as an opportunity to not only integrate what the stop destroying video games petition (which has over a million signatures) wants. But this act could also look at what are called "digital rights management" systems (DRM). These systems check whether or not your purchase is legitimate. The irony is that these systems require an internet connection and may be lost if servers shut down. Ironically these systems are easily cracked and lead to a better experience for people who illegally obtain (pirate) the games that people who buy the games legally. Often these systems false flag legally purchased copies and hamper performance. DRM's mostly only serve as a show to investors of companies. These systems also appeared on a Polish train where it would break because of this type of system. This also leads to the stop destroying video games petition as most DRM's rely on servers and these eventually will get shut down. Thank you for reading.
